text: In recent years, the field of artificial intelligence (AI) has undergone a profound paradigm shift driven by the transformative capabilities of generative models across both supervised and unsupervised learning frameworks. Generative AI has achieved state-of-the-art results in addressing complex real-world challenges, spanning image-to-image translation, medical diagnostics, multimodal synthesis, and natural language processing. This paper provides a systematic review and analysis of recent technical advancements in generative modeling, offering an in-depth discussion of domain-specific architectures and their practical implementations. We specifically highlight the revolutionary impact of Large Language Models (LLMs) and diffusion-based image synthesis, alongside emerging interdisciplinary applications. The primary contribution of this work is a coherent synthesis of the current landscape, weaving together contemporary breakthroughs to provide a roadmap for the future trajectory of the field. Finally, we address the critical importance of Responsible AI principles, examining the ethical considerations necessary to ensure the sustainable growth and societal integration of generative technologies.
